rows.notes: Top-left folio/date heading is legible ('43 To Dec:31.1900.'). No monetary columns present.
Large centered title 'Receipts' is very clear; no amounts on title line.
Right-of-center page title 'General Acct.' is crisp and unambiguous.
Section header underlined; no monetary amount on the header line.

Pounds read as '2583', shillings '12', pence '3' in right-most columns. Slight ambiguity between a 5 or 6 in the hundreds-place, but digit shapes favor '2583'. Column alignment with neighbouring rows consistent.
Ditto-marked 'Houses' line shows pounds '1019' and shillings '1'; pence column appears blank (interpreted as 0). The pence area is faint here so pence set to 0 with moderate confidence.
Numbers read clearly as '111 3 4' in the three currency columns; handwriting is neat for this line.
Very small amount in pounds column appears as '2' and shillings '7'. Pence column faint/blank so set to 0. The description contains quotes and is slightly cramped.
Aligned numerals read as '499 6 2'. Column boundaries unambiguous; digits clear.
Pounds read '2414' and shillings '2'; pence column lightly stroked/blank so interpreted as 0. Slight smudge on shillings digit causing moderate uncertainty.
Small amount reads clearly as '17 10' (pounds/shillings). Pence column blank. Vertical alignment with a subtotal mark immediately to the right suggests this is the last item before a subtotal.

Underlined subtotal to the right of the Timber line reads '3249 12' (pence blank). This is visually a subtotal for the earlier A. External items; digits are a bit crowded by the underline so moderate confidence.
Large clear figure '6781 8 1' aligned in the main currency columns; handwriting and alignment are unambiguous.
Small amount '48 3' is legible. Pence column blank; interpreted as 0. Reasonable clarity though the line is shorter than others.
Section header 'B. Internal' underlined and clear; no amounts on this line.
Clear numerals '241 17 6' aligned consistently in currency columns; handwriting is neat and distinct.
Reads as '242 10' with pence column blank; digits are well-formed and alignment clear.
Large amount clearly written as '3616 17 9' in the £/s/d columns; column guides line up with neighboring rows.
Numeric sequence '2906 9 6' is clear and well aligned; very high legibility.
Reads '1422 1' with pence blank; column alignment and handwriting for pounds are crisp.
Description 'Other sources: King's Charles I.' visible. Amount reads '167 4 8' though the description is slightly cramped; numeric columns are clear.
Amount to right reads '2217 14' (pence blank). The description appears to read 'Profit on Private Bills' but the script is condensed; moderate confidence in the textual reading and pence assumed 0.
Underlined section total clearly printed as '8619 17 9' at right of B Internal; digits are distinct and alignment is unambiguous.
Section header 'C. From Trust Funds' underlined and clearly visible; no amounts.

Small amount in pounds column reads '19'. It is ambiguous whether this is pounds or shillings in older ledgers; because it sits in the pounds column it is recorded as £19 0s 0d. Description spelling 'Symers' is inferred from script; moderate confidence.
Clear reading of '415 6 8' in currency columns; description 'King Charles I. Fund' legible.

Handwritten description is somewhat stylized; read here as 'Vefree Exhibition' (script could be 'V[?]e Exhibition' or 'Keffe Exhibition'). Amount '77 6 8' is clear. Text uncertainty reduces confidence.
Entry 'Mitchell' followed by ditto mark. Amount reads '39' in pounds column; shillings/pence blank (set to 0). Description legible.
Amount clearly reads '67 13 4'. Description 'Gifford' clear; quotation mark indicates repetition of preceding label formatting.
Read as '65 6 8' in currency columns; description 'Lowcombe Richards' legible though script is cursive.

Line shows 'Dr Richards' but the amount area appears to have quotation marks or is left blank (ditto marks). It is unclear whether an amount is intended here or the ditto refers to prior figure. Because no clear numerals appear, amounts left blank with low confidence.
Amount '77 6 8' matches earlier similarly formatted scholarship items; description inferred as 'Carter' from cursive 'Carter'/'Cuter' shape—moderate confidence in spelling.
Reads as '130 13 4' in numeric columns. The name looks like 'Husker' or possibly 'Hunter'; the first letter is slightly ambiguous, so textual confidence reduced.
Amount '305 8 4' clearly visible. Description reads as 'Thorbridge Trust (for Library)' or similar; the parenthetical 'for Library' is legible. Good numeric clarity.
Underlined subtotal for C '1197 1 8' is clearly printed in the right columns; digits and underline are unambiguous.
Final grand total at bottom-right reads clearly as '£13688 12 6'. Very legible and aligned as the page total.
